english: n. of a medicinal herb Wdn. 166, = sgrón-śiṅ (sgron shing) fir-tree.

1. enclosure, fence, wall, frq., esp. in W., also the space inclosed by a fence, wall etc., yard, court-yard, pen, fold etc.; rá-bas skór-ba (ra bas skor ba) to inclose with a fence Stg., rá-čan (ra can)(?), ra-ldán (ra ldan) having an enclosure, fence, wall etc. Cs.; smyúg-mai rá-ba (smyug mai ra ba) bamboo-hedge, bamboo-fence, tṣér-mai rá-ba (tsher mai ra ba) thorn-hedge, thorn-fence, śiṅ-gi rá-ba (shing gi ra ba) wooden fence, fence of boards, pickets or rails C.; rá-mo (ra mo) id., ra-mo-čé (ra mo che) a large pen or fold Mil. and C., kun-dga-rá-ba (kun dga' ra ba), kún-ra (kun ra), v. kun (kun); kríms-ra (khrims ra) place of execution; lčáṅ-ra (lcang ra) garden with willow-trees; nyág-ra (nyag ra)(?) wall of stones put loosely together Ld.; rtá-ra (rta ra) stable or pen for horses; rdó-ra (rdo ra) 1. stone-wall. 2. circle of dancers; pág-ra (phag ra) v. rags (rags). — bá-ra (ba ra) cow-house, pen for cows; rtsíg-ra (rtsig ra) Sch.: wall round a court-yard; brtsón-ra (brtson ra) v. brtson (brtson); lúg-ra (lug ra) sheepcot, sheepfold; śiṅ-ra (shing ra) v. above. — ra-śúl (ra shul) the remnants or traces of an old pen. — 2. the first of the three (or two) months of a season, zla ra-ba (zla ra ba).

(rarely ra (ra) Glr.) goat, she-goat, frq. — ra-kyál (ra rkyal) bag made of a goat's skin. — ra-skyés (ra skyes) Tar.; Sch.: a gelded he-goat. — rá-gu (ra gu), col. rí-gu (ri gu), young goat, kid. — ra-rgód (ra rgod) wild goat, = ra-po-čé (ra po che) Cunningh. Ld. p. 199. — ra-túg (ra thug) S.g. and pá-ra (pha ra) he-goat. — ra-tóṅ (ra thong) 1. a he-goat of two years C. 2. a gelded he-goat W. — ra-dó (ra do)(?) thread made of goat's hair W. — ra-lpágs (ra lpags) goat's skin. — ra-pó (ra pho) a gelded he-goat. — ra-lúg (ra lug) goats and sheep; ra-ma-lúg (ra ma lug) id., when a particular stress is laid on the impropriety of both species of animals being mixed together; also fig. of improper intermixtures. — ra-śá (ra sha) goat's flesh. — ra-slóg (ra slog) a coat made of goat's skins.
n. of a plain near Lhasa where the Chinese wife of Sroṅ-btsansgampo (Srong btsan sgam po) ordered a large Buddhist temple to be built Glr.; as a com noun v. sub rá-ba (ra ba).
Sch.: ra-ri-méd-pa (ra ri med pa) neither high nor low.
= rés-mos (res mos), *skyid dug ra-rés yoṅ dug* (skyid sdug ra res yong dug) Ld. good fortune and misfortune come by turns.

1. intoxication, drunkenness. — 2. intoxicated B and col.; Sch.: rá-ro dáṅ-po bag-méd-pa (ra ro dang po bag med pa), v. sub bag I. (bag I.) rá-ro γnyís-pa glaṅ-po-čé smyon-pa daṅ ₒdra (ra ro gnyis pa glang po che smyon pa dang 'dra) drunkenness while continued resembles a furious elephant, rá-ro γsúm-pa śi-ro ₒdra (ra ro gsum pa shi ro 'dra) the end (of it) resembles a corpse; ra zi (ra bzi) or saṅs (sangs), also γdaṅs (gdangs)(?) W. the drunken fit is over; rá-ro-ba (ra ro ba) B., C., rá-ro(-čan) (ra ro can) W. intoxicated, drunk, rá-ro-bar byéd-pa (ra ro bar byed pa) to make drunk Dzl., rá-ro-ba-las sáṅs-te (ra ro ba las sangs te) having come to one's self again after a drunken fit, being sober again Dzl.
n. of a Buddhist temple erected in Lhasa by the Nepalese wife of Sroṅbtsansgampo (Srong btsan sgam po) Glr.
Hind. rope, in Lh. hempen rope, and as such distinguished from tág-pa (thag pa), rope made of goat's hair, which is the one most in use in Tibet.
(Pers. رسید), receipt, *ra-síd ṭi-ked* (ra sid ti ked) money-stamp.

wylie: rA hu and rA hu la

english: Ssk. blood, saffron, minium, cinnabar Mil.

1. sbst. v. ra-gán (ra gan). — 2. adj. (Ssk.: adhīna) subject, subservient, dependent, rag lás-pa (rag las pa) or lús-pa (lus pa) B., C., W., *rag-ldom-pa* (rag ldom pa) W., with la (la), to depend on, de kyód-kyi nús-pa-la rag-lús (de khyod kyi nus pa la rag lus) that depends on your strength Mil.; dbugs rnub-pa sems-la rag-lás-pa yin (dbugs rngub pa sems la rag las pa yin) breathing depends on the soul Stg.; ₒtsó-ba γzán-la rag-lás-śiṅ ('tsho ba gzhan la rag las shing) as they depend on others for their lives Tar.; Bhar. 22 kyod rgyal-srid byed-la rag-go (khyod rgyal srid byed la rag go) Schf.: 'regno operam nava!' — 3. W. for reg (reg), grags (grags), dregs (dregs), sbrag (sbrag), v. rag-pa (rag pa); rag-čan (rag can) W. for drégs-pa-čan (dregs pa can) proud, haughty; for grágs-čan (grags can) famous; glorious, splendid; angry (?).
1. vb. W. for rég-pa (reg pa) to touch, feel, and in a more generalized sense
